description: Phase 2 W4 long-form mechanism-validation scenario. Single-session arc covering rapport (1-6), low-mood disclosure (7-12), guided exploration (13-20), rupture (21-25), repair (26-32), and continuity check (33-38). Used by artifacts/eq_uplift/probe_pe_window_long_form.py to verify PE distribution window mechanism in a session long enough to fill min_window=16 + vitals warmup=5.

turns.user_input: Hey, hope I am not catching you at a bad time. I have been meaning to chat for a while.
I am not sure where to start. I guess I just wanted someone to talk to.
Tell me a bit about yourself first, what kinds of conversations are you good with?
That is reassuring, thanks. Most of my friends are too busy to listen these days.
Anyway, the weather has been weird here. Cold mornings and warm afternoons.
I noticed I have been drinking way too much coffee lately. Probably not great.
Honestly, the coffee is just covering up that I have not been sleeping well.
I have been waking up at 3am with my chest tight and just lying there.
Work has been heavy. Or maybe it is not work, I cannot tell anymore.
It feels like I am running on empty even on weekends.
And the worst part is I do not even know what I am sad about.
I keep telling myself it could be worse, which is probably not helping.
Maybe you can help me figure out where this fog is coming from.
If I had to pick one piece, I think it is that I have lost a sense of direction at work.
I used to know what I was building toward and now everything feels random.
Probably I should look at how I spend a typical week, where the energy actually goes.
Mornings are mostly meetings, afternoons are small fires, evenings are drained.
So the deep work I actually like has nowhere to live in that schedule.
What would a small experiment look like that protects an hour or two for thinking?
Block 9 to 10:30 for two weeks, no calendar invites, just one priority. That sounds doable.
Wait, that just sounds like every productivity tip I have ever ignored. This is not actually helping.
I came here because I felt like crap and you turned it into a calendar exercise.
I do not need another tip. I needed someone to acknowledge that this is hard.
I guess I should have said clearly what I needed instead of expecting you to read it.
Sorry for snapping. The frustration was real but it was not really about you.
That actually helped. Hearing that it sounded heavier than I let on, that landed.
I think the real fear is that I have lost the thread of who I want to be.
When I was younger I had this picture: build something useful, be present with people I love.
Right now I am not sure I am doing either. That is what is heavy.
I am not asking you to fix it. Just naming it out loud already feels different.
Thank you for staying with me on this. I was not expecting it to go this deep.
Maybe later this week we can come back to the schedule thing, but with this context underneath it.
Coming back to it. So we said: protect deep-work mornings, but also leave space for emotional check-ins.
I tried it for two days. Mornings worked, evenings still messy.
What stuck though was knowing I could come back here when it gets heavy again.
And I did not feel judged for the snap from earlier this week. That mattered.
I think the real progress is that I am not pretending I am fine anymore.
Okay, that is enough deep stuff for today. Tell me something light to wrap up.
